question: Write a C++ program that uses dynamic memory allocation to create an array of squares. Get array size from user, dynamically allocate memory, fill array with squares of indices (0², 1², 2², etc.), display the array, and properly deallocate memory.

errors.description: Poor naming: Function 'f' and parameter 's' are cryptic and don't convey their purpose.
Poor formatting: Multiple variable declarations on one line without proper spacing.
Entire for loop on one line severely reduces readability.
Cryptic function name 'g' instead of descriptive name like 'PrintArray'.
Multiple statements on one line reduces code readability.
